revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24699703697|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Futamata-jinja (Iwaki)]][[ja:二俣神社 (いわき市)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Futamata Shrine | native_name = 二俣神社 | image = Futamata Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Futamata Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|37.105634|140.874302}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|lt=Ōjin|ar|الإمبراطور أوجين|arz|الامبراطور اوجين|azb|اوجین (ایمپیراتور)|bn|সম্রাট ওজিন|cs|Ódžin|de|Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|es|Ōjin Tennō|eu|Ōjin enperadorea|fa|امپراتور اوجین|fi|Ōjin|fr|Ōjin|hr|Ōjin|id|Kaisar Ōjin|it|Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|ka|ოძინი|ko|오진 천황|nl|Ojin|pl|Ōjin|pt|Ojin|ru|Император Одзин|sh|Car Ōjin|simple|Emperor Ōjin|sl|Cesar Odžin|sv|Ojin|th|จักรพรรดิโอจิง|tl|Emperador Ōjin|tr|Ōjin (imparator)|uk|Імператор Одзін|uz|Imperator Ōjin|vi|Thiên hoàng Ōjin|yo|Emperor Ōjin|zh_min_nan|Ôzin Thian-hông|zh_yue|應神天皇|zh|应神天皇|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | established = {'time': '+0860-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} }} {{nihongo|'''Futamata Shrine'''|二俣神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mutsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|ja|陸奥国の式内社一覧|qid=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 36 ** P155: {{ill|Q11433670|ltq=Q11433670|qid=Q11433670|1=Ōkunitama Shrine (Iwaki)}} ** P156: {{ill|Onsen Shrine|lt=Onsen Shrine|ja|温泉神社 (いわき市)|qid=Q54153253|1=Onsen Shrine (Iwaki)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q274384|ltq=Q274384|qid=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}} * {{ill|Q907389|ltq=Q907389|qid=Q907389|1=Mutsu Province}} * {{ill|Iwaki district|lt=Iwaki district|ja|石城郡|qid=Q11585428|1=Iwaki district}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|37.105634|140.874302}} == inception (P571) == * {'time': '+0860-00-00T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 9, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985786'} == image (P18) == * Futamata Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Q317997|ltq=Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182073 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * ふたまたじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3380005006299 == Commons category (P373) == * Futamata-jinja (Iwaki) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '福島県いわき市小川町下小川字梅ノ作53-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Futamata-jinja (Iwaki)}} [[JA:二俣神社 (いわき市)]] {{wikidata link|Q63147989}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name = Futamata Shrine |Image = [[File:Futamata Jinja Iwaki Haiden.jpg|270px|]]
Haiden (worship hall) |Location = {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|qid=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Ogawa-machi Shimogawa|Q128847384|qid=Q128847384|1=Ogawa-machi Shimogawa}} Umenosaku 53-1 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin}} |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |Founded = First year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|qid=Q24714|1=860}}) |Main hall = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} copper-sheet roof |Alternate name = Futamata Hachimangū |Pilgrimage status = |Annual festival = First Saturday and Sunday of September |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Fukushima|label=Futamata Shrine|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Futamata Jinja Iwaki Iriguchi.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Shrine grounds entrance}}]] '''Futamata Shrine''' (ふたまたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Fukushima Prefecture|Q71707|qid=Q71707|1=Fukushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Iwaki City|Q274384|qid=Q274384|1=Iwaki, Fukushima}}{{ill|lt=Ogawa-machi Shimogawa|Q128847384|qid=Q128847384|1=Ogawa-machi Shimogawa}}. It is a {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=shikinaisha (minor shrine)|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank system|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a gōsha. === History === According to shrine tradition, it was established by enshrinement in the first year of {{ill|lt=Jōgan (Japan)|Q1075635|qid=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} ({{ill|lt=860|Q24714|qid=Q24714|1=860}}). In the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, it is listed as one of the seven shrines of {{ill|lt=Ishiki District|Q11585428|qid=Q11585428|lt=Iwaki District|1=Iwaki district}}, ranked as a minor shrine. In the second year of {{ill|lt=Shōō|Q5551|qid=Q5551|1=1289}} ({{ill|lt=1289|Q5551|qid=Q5551|1=1289}}), {{ill|lt=Ogawa Muneyoshi|Q132431591|qid=Q132431591|1=Ogawa Muneyoshi}} (also known as Ogawa Yoshitsuna), the second son of {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshitane|Q60990135|qid=Q60990135|1=Satake Yoshitane}}, who had advanced into this region, restored the main shrine. === From the Edo period onward === It is said that the shrine originally stood at the site of Kawakami Hachimangū, located a little over 1 km northwest of the present location. However, due to frequent flood damage, it was relocated to its current site in the second year of {{ill|lt=Tenna (Japan)|Q1145289|qid=Q1145289|lt=Tenna|1=Tenna}} ({{ill|lt=1682|Q7648|qid=Q7648|1=1682}}) by {{ill|lt=Iwaki-Taira Domain|Q843782|qid=Q843782|1=Iwakitaira Domain}} lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshiyasu|Q8514975|qid=Q8514975|1=Naitō Yoshimune}}. In the 11th year of {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} ({{ill|lt=1698|Q7706|qid=Q7706|1=1698}}), shrine building repairs were carried out by domain lord {{ill|lt=Naitō Yoshitaka|Q8514986|qid=Q8514986|1=Naitō Yoshitaka}}, and thereafter repairs continued under the patronage of successive domain lords. In the fourth year of {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} ({{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}, and in the seventh year of Meiji ({{ill|lt=1874|Q7761|qid=Q7761|1=1874}}) the present shrine buildings were constructed. === Shrine grounds === Within the grounds is the '''Umenosaku tile kiln site''', where tiles were produced for the Natsui Haiji temple ruins (Iwaki City {{ill|lt=Taira Shimogōe|Q128846875|qid=Q128846875|1=Taira Shimogōe}}). The Natsui Haiji temple was located downstream along the Natsui River, and it is thought that the tiles were loaded onto boats and transported down the river. File:Futamata Jinja Iwaki Honden.jpg|Main hall File:Futamata Jinja Iwaki Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall === Access === * {{ill|lt=East Japan Railway Company|Q499071|qid=Q499071|1=East Japan Railway Company}} (JR East) {{ill|lt=Banetsu East Line|Q807037|qid=Q807037|1=Ban'etsu East Line}} (Yūyū Abukuma Line): 30 minutes on foot from either “{{ill|lt=Akai Station|Q4700176|qid=Q4700176|1=Akai Station}}” or “{{ill|lt=Ogawagō Station|Q7079855|qid=Q7079855|1=Ogawagō Station}}” (the shrine is located almost midway between the two stations). === Notes === {{Reflist}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province|Q11658590|qid=Q11658590|1=List of Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province}} === External links === * [http://engishiki.org/mutu/bun/mut281202-01.html Futamata Shrine] {{Shinto Navbox}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Futamata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Futamata Shrine]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:福島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Iwaki, Fukushima]] [[Category:History of Iwaki]]
